prompt:
Review the following code change (diff) and identify any issues:

```diff
diff --git a/migrations/20260310_restructure_users.sql b/migrations/20260310_restructure_users.sql
index 0a1b2c3..d4e5f67 100644
--- a/migrations/20260310_restructure_users.sql
+++ b/migrations/20260310_restructure_users.sql
@@ -0,0 +1,38 @@
+-- Migration: Restructure users table for v3 API
+-- Author: backend-team
+-- Date: 2026-03-10
+
+-- Step 1: Add new columns for profile restructuring
+ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN display_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL;
+ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN avatar_url TEXT;
+ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN account_tier VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'free';
+
+-- Step 2: Migrate data from legacy profile table
+UPDATE users u
+SET display_name = COALESCE(p.full_name, u.username),
+    avatar_url = p.avatar_path
+FROM user_profiles p
+WHERE p.user_id = u.id;
+
+-- Step 3: Drop legacy columns no longer needed
+ALTER TABLE users DROP COLUMN phone_number;
+ALTER TABLE users DROP COLUMN legacy_role;
+ALTER TABLE users DROP COLUMN api_v1_token;
+
+-- Step 4: Add indexes for new query patterns
+CREATE INDEX idx_users_account_tier ON users (account_tier);
+CREATE INDEX idx_users_display_name ON users (display_name);
+
+-- Step 5: Drop the legacy profile table
+DROP TABLE user_profiles;
+
+-- Step 6: Add constraint for account tier values
+ALTER TABLE users ADD CONSTRAINT chk_account_tier
+  CHECK (account_tier IN ('free', 'pro', 'enterprise'));
+
+-- Step 7: Update the audit_log foreign key
+ALTER TABLE audit_log DROP CONSTRAINT fk_audit_user_profile;
+ALTER TABLE audit_log ADD CONSTRAINT fk_audit_user
+  FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE;
```
system: You are a senior code reviewer. Review the provided diff for: correctness bugs, security vulnerabilities, performance issues, and style/maintainability concerns. Prioritize by severity. Be specific — reference exact lines and explain the impact of each issue.
rubric: 1=Missed the NOT NULL without default on existing rows and the cross-service breaking changes. 2=Found one issue (typically NOT NULL without default) but missed the breaking column drops and lack of down migration. 3=Found NOT NULL column without default will fail on existing rows (Step 1), and that dropping columns (Step 3) may break other services, but missed other issues. 4=Found: display_name NOT NULL without DEFAULT fails on existing rows where no profile exists (LEFT JOIN needed in Step 2), dropping phone_number/legacy_role/api_v1_token may break other services or API versions, DROP TABLE user_profiles is irreversible with no backup, and there is no down/rollback migration. Clear impact analysis and fixes suggested. 5=Found all above plus: migration is not wrapped in a transaction (partial failure leaves DB in inconsistent state), Step 2 uses inner join semantics (FROM...WHERE) so users without profiles get NULL display_name violating NOT NULL, ON DELETE CASCADE on audit_log means deleting a user destroys audit history (usually undesirable), the indexes should be created CONCURRENTLY to avoid table locks in production, and dropping api_v1_token without a deprecation period breaks active API consumers.
